name: Can flooring be installed around kitchen islands and cabinets?

mainEntity.name: Can you install flooring under kitchen cabinets or around a kitchen island?
mainEntity.text: Can you install flooring under kitchen cabinets or around a kitchen island?

acceptedAnswer.text: Whether flooring goes under cabinets or around a kitchen island depends on the product and installation method. Floating floors usually need to move independently, so trapping them under heavy cabinets can cause buckling or prevent proper expansion. Some glue-down or tile systems can be installed under cabinetry, but that decision must follow the manufacturer’s instructions and the cabinet schedule. Kitchen islands create a similar issue because the floor edge must be detailed carefully around fixed objects. If the island is already installed, the contractor may need to cut precise openings and use trim or transitions. If the island is not yet installed, sequencing the work correctly is important. Ask the installer how they will handle appliance gaps, toe kicks, and cabinet clearances. A finished kitchen floor should look clean, but it also has to move and perform correctly under everyday use. The wrong sequence can create a permanent problem that is expensive to repair later.
acceptedAnswer.description: Flooring can sometimes run under cabinets or around islands, but it depends on the flooring type, cabinet weight, and manufacturer rules. Many floating floors should not be trapped under heavy fixed cabinets because they need room to move.
